item.title: Exclusive: Musk rewrites IPO playbook with large slice of SpaceX stock for retail investors, source says
item.description: Elon Musk is discussing allocating as much as 30% of SpaceX’s initial public offering to individual investors — at least three ​times the usual retail slice — leaning on his rabid fan base and other loyal backers to help steady the stock after its debut, a person ‌familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Trump officials tout US energy dominance as global oil execs warn of supply crisis
item.description: U.S. officials this week said the historic spike in fuel prices during the war in Iran will be short-term and trumpeted record U.S. production at an industry conference where oil executives and government officials ​from Asia, the Middle East and Europe bemoaned the worst oil and gas supply disruption in decades.

item.title: CERAWEEK Private firms spearhead global shale push as US public operators stay home
item.description: Private energy companies and their backers are emerging as leaders ​in the next phase of global shale development, taking early positions in overseas basins while publicly listed U.S. producers focus on capital discipline and their core ‌domestic acreage.

item.title: Microsoft freezes hiring in major cloud, sales groups, The Information reports
item.description: Microsoft executives ​have in recent ‌weeks told managers ​at ​major divisions, including ⁠its ​cloud unit ​and North American sales groups, ​to ​suspend new hiring, ‌The ⁠Information reported on Thursday, citing ​three ​employees ⁠with direct ​knowledge of ​the ⁠decision.

item.title: Musk urges judge to review Twitter verdict, accuses jury of 'mocking' him
item.description: A lawyer ​for Elon Musk urged a federal judge to review a recent jury verdict finding that Musk ‌defrauded Twitter investors when buying the social media company, saying jurors improperly used the verdict to "send a message" by finding him liable.

item.title: Bank of Canada says it will have a tough job tackling structural changes
item.description: By Promit Mukherjee and David Ljunggren

item.title: United Airlines reaches tentative labor deal with flight attendants
item.description: United ​Airlines flight ‌attendants have reached ​a ​new tentative labor ⁠agreement ​with the ​company, their union said ​on ​Thursday.

item.title: Crypto platform Anchorage brings Sun's Tron to US investors
item.description: Anchorage Digital, a federally regulated U.S. crypto platform, said on Thursday it would add Justin Sun's Tron ​blockchain to its network, expanding the venture's access to U.S. ‌investors.

item.title: Stryker says manufacturing mostly restored after cyberattack
item.description: Medical device maker Stryker said on Thursday its operations were steadily improving towards full ​capacity, after a cyberattack caused widespread ‌disruption to its business.

item.title: Meta shares slip after US jury verdicts raise concerns of new legal exposure
item.description: Meta shares fell 6% to a 10-month low on Thursday after rulings this week found the Facebook parent failed to adequately warn or ​protect young users, stoking concerns about billions of dollars in fines ‌from new cases and follow-on litigation.

item.title: Peso mexicano se negocia estable atento a Medio Oriente, aguarda decisión Banxico
item.description: El peso mexicano mostraba pocos cambios el jueves, mientras que la bolsa caía en un mercado sin rumbo debido a la cautela de los ​inversionistas atentos a noticias sobre la posibilidad de un pronto fin del conflicto ‌en Medio Oriente.

item.title: Dutch court orders xAI, Grok not to create, distribute nonconsensual sex images in Netherlands
item.description: A Dutch ‌court ​on ​Thursday ordered ⁠Elon ​Musk's xAI ​and chatbot ​Grok ​not to generate ‌and ⁠distribute sexualized ​images ​of ⁠people ​without ​their ⁠consent in ⁠the ​Netherlands.

item.title: US fixed 30-year mortgage rate jumps to six-month high as Iran war drags on
item.description: The average rate on the popular U.S. 30-year fixed-rate mortgage has ​jumped to a six-month high as ‌rising oil prices from the dragging Middle East war fanned inflation worries, which could ​weigh on home sales during the ​typically busy spring season.

item.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit by Musk's X Corp accusing advertisers of illegal boycott
item.description: A U.S. judge ​on Thursday dismissed ‌X Corp's antitrust lawsuit that accused ​the World ​Federation of Advertisers and ⁠a group ​of major companies ​including Mars, CVS Health and Colgate-Palmolive of illegally ​boycotting billionaire ​entrepreneur Elon Musk’s social ‌media ⁠company.

item.title: Novo Nordisk appoints Mars CEO as board observer
item.description: Novo Nordisk on Thursday elected Poul Weihrauch, CEO ​of family-owned U.S. candy and pet food ‌giant Mars, as observer to its board, as the drugmaker seeks to strengthen its position in the highly ​competitive U.S. obesity market.

item.title: Tom Brady-backed telehealth firm eMed valued at over $2 billion in latest funding round
item.description: Telehealth firm eMed said on Thursday it has raised $200 million in its latest funding round, ​valuing the Miami, Florida-based company at more than $2 billion.

item.title: Blackstone invests $250 million in UAE despite war disruption
item.description: Blackstone has invested $250 million in Advanced Digital Gaming Technology, a new payments and data intelligence technology platform based in the ​United Arab Emirates, the asset manager said in a statement ‌on Thursday.

item.title: GlobalFoundries files patent infringement lawsuits against Tower Semiconductor
item.description: GlobalFoundries on ​Thursday said it has sued Israel-based rival ‌Tower Semiconductor , alleging that Tower Semiconductor infringed on 11 GlobalFoundries patents that relate to manufacturing chips that go ​into smart phones and other electronic ​devices.

item.title: Bank of Montreal targets 15% return on equity on wealth, US businesses growth
item.description: Bank of Montreal said on Thursday it is targeting a return on equity ​of more than 15% by 2028, as it eyes ‌growth across its wealth management and U.S. businesses.

item.title: Mexico announces new measures to modernize the motor transport industry
item.description: The Mexican ​government announced on ‌Thursday a new set of measures aimed ​to support ​the country's motor transport ⁠industry.

item.title: Defense technology startup Shield AI valued at $12.7 billion in latest funding round
item.description: Shield AI is raising $2 billion in a new funding round at a $12.7 billion valuation, as the defense technology startup looks ​to expand its artificial intelligence-powered software amid growing use ‌of autonomous tools during conflicts.

item.title: Russian farmers complain of mobile internet shutdowns amid spring planting
item.description: Russian government measures to shut down mobile internet across the country because of Ukrainian ​drone attacks are affecting fieldwork during the ‌planting season, a major farming lobby said on Thursday.

item.title: European Payments Initiative CEO says Trump fears are boosting its appeal
item.description: Fears that ​President Donald Trump's administration could limit Europe's access to U.S. payments infrastructure are boosting regional ‌adoption of a competing European payment platform, the CEO of the European Payments Initiative (EPI) told Reuters.

item.title: Apple adds Bosch, Cirrus Logic, others to US manufacturing program, to invest $400 million
item.description: Apple said it was adding Bosch, Cirrus Logic , TDK ​and Qnity Electronics to its American Manufacturing ‌Program, with plans to invest $400 million through 2030 to expand U.S.-based production of key components.

item.title: Two senators call for US tariff probe into construction and farm equipment
item.description: Two U.S. senators, a Democrat and a Republican, urged the Commerce Department on Thursday to launch a national security investigation ​into the import of heavy construction and farm equipment made in Mexico ‌by companies such as Deere & Co , Caterpillar and CNH Industrial .

item.title: US weekly jobless claims increase slightly
item.description: New applications for U.S. unemployment benefits rose slightly last week, suggesting the labor market remains stable and giving the Federal Reserve scope to hold interest rates steady while monitoring ​inflation risks linked to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: European retailers warn of price shock, weaker demand from prolonged Middle East conflict
item.description: Europe's retailers warned on Thursday that a prolonged Middle East conflict could push up sticker prices in Europe and ‌dent consumer demand for companies from clothing giant H&M to British supermarket chain Co-op .

item.title: Crypto for a home? Coinbase brings token-backed down payments to housing market
item.description: It's one of the most ambitious attempts yet ​to adapt digital assets for mainstream needs.

item.title: Iran war chokes petrochemical supply, sends plastic prices soaring
item.description: The Middle East accounted for over 40% of polyethylene exports in 2025.

item.title: German deepfake porn case sparks protests and pressure for change in law
item.description: Germany's ​government is facing pressure to toughen laws against digital violence after a prominent television actor accused her former husband of ‌posting AI-generated porn resembling her on fake online accounts purporting to belong to her.
